source: Derived from error patterns across CB SAT R&W practice (data/cb-corpus/)

mistakeCategories.name: Contrast word where continuation is needed (or vice-versa)
mistakeCategories.description: Student picks 'however' when the sentences agree, or 'furthermore' when they contrast.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Two agreeing sentences joined with 'In contrast'.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Offer a transition signaling the OPPOSITE relationship (contrast vs continuation) from the correct one.

mistakeCategories.name: Cause/effect connector with no causal link
mistakeCategories.description: Student picks 'therefore'/'because' when the relationship is merely sequential or additive.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Adds 'as a result' between two unrelated facts.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Offer a cause-effect transition ('therefore', 'consequently') where none is justified.

mistakeCategories.name: Example/emphasis word that doesn't fit
mistakeCategories.description: Student picks 'for example' or 'in fact' where the next sentence isn't an example or intensification.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: 'For instance' before a sentence that gives no instance.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Offer an illustration/emphasis transition that doesn't match the next sentence's role.
